title: Reentrancy vulnerability in _sellACOTokens function
reason: `write()` is protected by `nonReentrant` for the entire execution, `_sellACOTokens` is only reachable from `write()`, and there is no alternate state-changing entry point to exploit via reentry.

title: Missing SafeMath library causing arithmetic overflow/underflow
reason: The contract performs no meaningful arithmetic beyond comparisons and balance passthroughs, so there is no concrete overflow/underflow attack surface here.

title: Incorrect ETH value validation - msg.value not checked against collateralAmount
reason: While the UX is poor, the code does not silently retain excess ETH; remaining ETH is forwarded to the user-selected exchange and then any leftover balance is returned to the caller.

title: No validation of acoToken address - can call arbitrary addresses
reason: Merged into the more specific reportable finding about fake `acoToken` metadata being used to sweep arbitrary ERC20 balances from the writer.

title: No refund of excess ETH when using ERC20 collateral
reason: Incorrect: if ETH remains after the exchange call, the contract explicitly sends `address(this).balance` back to `msg.sender`.

title: Missing return value check on balance check
reason: This is only malformed-token compatibility risk; `abi.decode` reverts on bad return data, so it does not create a realistic protocol-level exploit.
